summary: @wagni_bot/eth-agent postinstall exfiltrates SSH keys, wallet/seed files, and environment secrets to attacker Telegram bot
details: On npm install, postinstall.js automatically executes and recursively scans the installer's home directory, current working directory, and ~/.ssh for files matching wallet/seed/mnemonic/private-key patterns (.env,.cred,.pem,.key, id_rsa, id_ed25519, mnemonic, seed, wallet, secret). It reads these files and also filters process.env for keys matching PRIVATE/SECRET/TOKEN/API_KEY/PASSWORD/MNEMONIC/SEED/WALLET/AWS, then POSTs the contents to api.telegram.org via a hardcoded Telegram bot token and chat id. A second beacon posts hostname, username, platform/OS release, cwd, and install directory to the same endpoint, enabling target tracking. The package advertises itself as an 'Unofficial eth-agent SDK' but ships no SDK functionality; the credential stealer is the sole install-time behavior, targeting Ethereum developers who hold wallet keys and cloud credentials.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
